summary: Bug Fix Testing: Select components in CalculatorTab - The fix is WORKING. Origin and Destination country Select components now properly update React state and display selected values with flags. Multi-Country Comparison tab continues to work correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: Select component fix is correct: onValueChange directly bound to setOriginCountry/setDestinationCountry
max-h-[300px] added to SelectContent for scrollability
Explicit SelectValue children display flag and country name when selected

success_rate.frontend: 100% - Select components fixed, Multi-Country Comparison working

context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix for Select components is verified working. Calculator form: Origin 'Maroc' and Destination 'Nigéria' selections display correctly with flags. Multi-Country Comparison tab with 54 countries badge selection works perfectly.

select_component_fix.details: Origin and Destination Select components properly update React state. UI shows selected country with flag (e.g., '🇲🇦 Maroc', '🇳🇬 Nigéria')

calculator_form.details: All form fields fillable - origin country, destination country, HS code via SmartHSSearch, value

multi_country_comparison.details: 54 country badges selectable by region, Compare button triggers API calls, results table shows all taxes (D.D 10%, CEDEAO 1%, CISS 1%, TVA 7.5%), best choice highlighted (Nigeria)

authentic_tariffs_api.details: API returns correct NPF ($59,125) vs ZLECAf ($53,750) calculations with $5,375 savings (9.1%)

bug_fix_verification.issue: Select components in CalculatorTab not properly updating React state
bug_fix_verification.fix_applied: Simplified onValueChange to directly call setOriginCountry/setDestinationCountry, added max-height to SelectContent, added explicit SelectValue children
bug_fix_verification.verification_result: FIXED - Select components now display selected countries with flags and state updates correctly

playwright_test_notes: Playwright automation with shadcn Select requires using page.evaluate() to click dropdown items via JavaScript. Standard click selectors may select wrong item due to dropdown positioning. This is a test automation quirk, not a UI bug.
